cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Start the morning wandering Campbell Farmers Market on Campbell Ave — pick up fresh fruit, grab a pastry from one of the bakery vendors, and ease into the day with a cup of coffee from a market stall.
cards.why: Sunday morning farmers markets are hard to beat when the weather is this good.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Triton Museum of Art and spend a couple hours taking in the contemporary exhibitions and the sculpture garden out back. It's a genuinely nice way to spend a late morning — free admission, no crowds, and real art.
cards.why: It's one of the most underrated spots in the South Bay and it's free.

cards.name: RAJARANI Bakery and Restaurant
cards.address: 952 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by RAJARANI Bakery and Restaurant on El Camino Real in Sunnyvale for lunch. Dig into the Indian-inspired menu and let yourself linger over something warm and satisfying.
cards.why: It's a solid, flavorful lunch that won't leave you dragging through the afternoon.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch on Stevens Creek Trail in Mountain View. It's flat and scenic, perfect for a relaxed Sunday stroll along the creek without any real effort required.
cards.why: 72 degrees and sunny is exactly the weather this trail was made for.

cards.name: Peet's Coffee
cards.address: 1875 S Bascom Ave Ste. 800, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with coffee and a pastry at Peet's — grab the kids a hot chocolate and settle in before the day gets going.
cards.why: Solid spot to fuel up before hitting the trail.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek path at Los Gatos Creek Trail — flat, shaded, and easy enough for little legs. Let the kids run ahead while you enjoy the morning air along the water.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a relaxed outdoor morning with the family.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Weekly open-air farmers market featuring local produce, artisan goods, and prepared foods.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: International Film Festival
cards.blurb: The Milpitas Library is celebrating international films in the month of April. We have curated a group of acclaimed movies ranging from a classic that won the O
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Sunday CrafterNoon
cards.blurb: Drop into Sunday CrafterNoon in Los Altos for some hands-on making with the kids. Free and fun, it's the kind of afternoon activity that keeps little ones genuinely engaged.
cards.why: Free, creative, and something the kids will actually talk about later.

cards.name: Nahita Bakery
cards.address: 1334 S Mary Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Nahita Bakery with a pastry and a proper coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: It's a genuinely good bakery and a low-key way to ease into a Sunday.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail along the water — it's shaded, flat, and easy to lose an hour on without trying.
cards.why: 62 degrees and overcast is basically perfect trail weather.

cards.name: Nature Book Club
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Drop into the Nature Book Club at the Los Gatos Library on Main St for a relaxed conversation about the natural world.
cards.why: It's free and exactly the kind of low-key Sunday thing that ends up being the best part of the day.

cards.name: Hapa's Brewing Company
cards.address: 114 S Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Hapa's Brewing Company on Santa Cruz Ave for lunch and a pint. It's a Los Gatos staple with solid food and a chill Sunday vibe.
cards.why: You're already in the neighborhood and this is exactly where you want to land after a morning walk.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. Walk the sculpture garden, then wander the contemporary galleries inside.
cards.why: Free admission and a sculpture garden you can actually enjoy on a mild afternoon like this.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row and grab a drink at one of the patio bars. This is the move for happy hour on a Sunday when the weather's nice.
cards.why: The outdoor seating hits differently on a warm evening with no agenda.

cards.name: Santana Row Chess Plaza
cards.address: 356 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Walk over to Santana Row Chess Plaza and settle in for a few games at the outdoor chess tables as the evening cools down.
cards.why: It's one of those spots that sounds random but ends up being a genuinely fun way to close out a Sunday.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at the Campbell Farmers Market — grab fresh fruit, pastries, and a coffee from the vendors, and let the kids pick out something fun to snack on while you browse.
cards.why: It's the perfect Sunday morning vibe and a great way to ease into the day with the whole family.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Los Gatos Creek Trail for a relaxed morning walk along the creek — shaded, flat, and easy for kids of all ages to handle without complaint.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the best possible window to get outside before it warms up.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Weekly open-air farmers market featuring local produce, artisan goods, and prepared foods.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: The Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum in San Jose is a total surprise — real mummies, actual artifacts, and hieroglyphics that make kids genuinely stop and stare. Walk through the replica tomb and let them soak it all in.
cards.why: It hits that sweet spot where kids think it's cool and parents think it's educational.

cards.name: Loto-Ja Ice Cream
cards.address: 74 S 24th St, San Jose, CA 95116, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Loto-Ja Ice Cream for a scoop before dinner — the kids have earned it and honestly so have you.
cards.why: Affordable, delicious, and a great way to keep everyone happy between the museum and dinner.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Santana Row is a great wind-down spot for the family. Walk the boulevard, let the kids people-watch, and grab dinner at one of the outdoor patio restaurants. Zinnia has a relaxed patio that works well with kids.
cards.why: The outdoor setting keeps restless kids happy while adults actually get to enjoy a proper sit-down meal.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Mountain View Farmers Market — grab a coffee and a pastry from one of the vendors, browse the produce, and ease into the day at a relaxed pace.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a slow Sunday morning outside.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the Stevens Creek Trail — it's flat, scenic, and easy to cover a few miles without breaking a sweat.
cards.why: Mid-50s morning air on a shaded trail is exactly where you want to be.

cards.name: Yiassoo
cards.address: 2180 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Yiassoo in Campbell does Greek fast-casual well — think gyros, falafel, and fresh sides that don't feel like an afterthought.
cards.why: Solid, affordable lunch without a long wait on a Sunday.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Los Gatos Creek Trail is a great post-lunch walk — shaded path along the creek, easy pace, and enough distance to stretch out the afternoon.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the nicest casual trails in the South Bay.

cards.name: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House | San Jose
cards.address: 1410 El Paseo de Saratoga, San Jose, CA 95130, USA
cards.blurb: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House is an interactive dinner experience where you cook thin-sliced wagyu in a hot broth at the table — go slow, order well, and enjoy it.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun dinner for two adults who want something a cut above the usual.

cards.name: Alyssa Daniels Senior Jazz Studies Recital at Music Building
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with Alyssa Daniels' Senior Jazz Studies Recital at the SJSU Music Building — free jazz on a Sunday evening is a rare and easy yes.
cards.why: Free live jazz is a great way to wind down without calling it a night too early.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Take the kids out on this flat, easy trail along the creek. Strollers roll great here, and older kids can run ahead without any stress.
cards.why: Perfect morning weather for a walk outside before the day heats up.

cards.name: Baby Wearing Dance
cards.blurb: Drop into this free baby wearing dance session and let the little ones wiggle around. It's a fun, low-pressure way to get some movement in with the kids.
cards.why: Free, kid-focused, and a genuinely fun mid-morning detour for families.

cards.name: DX Braised Chicken Rice 鼎香黄焖鸡
cards.address: 20803 Stevens Creek Blvd #110, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Sit down for a bowl of braised chicken rice. The portions are generous and the kids will love the savory, saucy chicken over rice.
cards.why: One of the most consistently great spots for a quick, filling, affordable lunch in the area.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake for the afternoon. Walk the easy trails around the lake, find a shady picnic spot, and let the kids ride the little train if it's running.
cards.why: This is exactly what a warm Sunday afternoon with kids should look like.

cards.name: Cafe Dolce Sicilia
cards.address: 1165 Lincoln Ave #110, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Cafe Dolce Sicilia is a tiny Willow Glen gem for a proper morning pastry and espresso — grab a seat and ease into the day before the crowds show up.
cards.why: It closes at 3 PM so morning is the move, and it's a genuinely great way to start a Sunday.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market lets you mix and match from a dozen vendors under one roof. Grab tacos from one stall, a banh mi from another, and settle into a patio seat with whatever looks best.
cards.why: It's downtown San Jose's best casual lunch situation, especially on a warm Sunday afternoon.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Triton Museum of Art is free contemporary art with a proper sculpture garden out back. Take your time with both the indoor galleries and the outdoor pieces.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a 75-degree Sunday afternoon is hard to beat.

cards.name: Sarika Thai Massage & Spa
cards.address: 1375 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Sarika Thai Massage and Spa on Lincoln Ave is the kind of afternoon treat you'll be glad you booked. Call ahead or walk in and let the day fully decompress.
cards.why: After a morning of museums, your body will thank you for this.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row's boulevard as the evening crowd picks up. Browse the boutiques, grab a drink at one of the outdoor patios, and start thinking about dinner.
cards.why: The energy on a Sunday evening here is just right for winding down in style.

cards.name: EDscapade Games
cards.address: 1150 Murphy Ave #200, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: EDscapade Games is San Jose's best escape room spot. Book a room for two and see how fast you can crack it.
cards.why: It's a great way to cap a Sunday with something genuinely fun and a little competitive.

cards.name: Daily Donuts & Sandwiches
cards.address: 110 S Sunnyvale Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with donuts and sandwiches — grab a dozen assorted donuts for the kids and a coffee drink for yourself while everyone wakes up.
cards.why: It's a low-key, affordable start that kids always love.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail along the water — it's flat, shaded, and totally stroller-friendly, so even the little ones can make it the whole way without complaints.
cards.why: Perfect morning weather for a creekside walk before it heats up.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours exploring real Egyptian mummies, ancient artifacts, and hieroglyphics at this genuinely surprising museum. Kids usually lose their minds over the mummy exhibit.
cards.why: It's the kind of place that makes kids ask questions the whole drive home.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Walk Japantown's main stretch, pick up some mochi or treats at Okayama Kobo, and grab a bowl of udon at Gombei for a casual dinner the kids will actually eat.
cards.why: It's one of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and a genuinely fun neighborhood to explore as a family.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Lace up and head up the Stanford foothills loop for sweeping views over the Bay. The climb is real but totally worth it, and the weather today is about as good as it gets for this trail.
cards.why: Perfect morning temperature for a hike before it warms up.

cards.name: Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto
cards.address: 368 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Post-hike, walk into the massage therapy center on California Ave and book a session to unknot everything the trail just did to your legs. They take walk-ins.
cards.why: You earned it after that climb.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a bánh mì or Vietnamese breakfast plate at SaiGon BreadFast on El Camino in Mountain View. It's a casual, fast lunch that punches way above its weight.
cards.why: Great fuel between stops and genuinely hard to beat for the price.

cards.name: Dogbotic Sound Petting Zoo
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Head over to 585 Franklin St in Mountain View for the Dogbotic Sound Petting Zoo, an interactive audio art experience happening today. Expect weird, tactile, sound-based installations you can actually touch and play with.
cards.why: It's free and genuinely unlike anything else you'd do on a Sunday afternoon.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into the Computer History Museum and wander through the full arc of computing history, from room-sized mainframes to the first Mac. Even if you're not a tech person, the sheer scale of what's in here is wild.
cards.why: It's one of the most underrated museums in the whole Bay Area and today's a great excuse to finally go.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Start the morning at the California Ave Farmers Market — let the kids pick out fruit, grab some fresh pastries, and wander the stalls while the day warms up.
cards.why: It's free, it's outside, and it's the perfect low-key way to kick off a Sunday with the family.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara for a free morning of contemporary art and a sculpture garden the kids can actually roam around in.
cards.why: Free admission and a real sculpture garden make this way more engaging for kids than it sounds.

cards.name: Sprouting Stories
cards.blurb: Sprouting Stories is a free family storytime event happening today in Los Altos — great for little ones who love books and songs.
cards.why: It's a free, structured activity designed exactly for kids, and it's happening today.

cards.name: The City Fish
cards.address: 21678 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at The City Fish on Stevens Creek — a casual, affordable spot to fuel up before the afternoon.
cards.why: Quick, easy on the wallet, and the kids won't complain about the menu.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the Stevens Creek Trail for a flat, easy afternoon walk or bike ride. It's wide open, stroller-friendly, and the kids can run without you worrying about traffic.
cards.why: The weather today is perfect for getting outside and burning off some of that lunch energy.

cards.name: Rick's Ice Cream & Pints of Joy
cards.address: 3946 Middlefield Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Rick's Ice Cream on Middlefield — scoop in hand, kids happy, mission accomplished.
cards.why: No better way to close out a full family Sunday than ice cream somewhere the whole crew can agree on.
